1	import warnings
2	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
3	from enum import Enum
4	
5	
6	class QuantType(Enum):
7	    BNB = "bnb"
8	    UNSLOTH = "unsloth" # dynamic 4-bit quantization
9	    GGUF = "GGUF"
10	    NONE = "none"
11	    BF16 = "bf16" # only for Deepseek V3
12	
13	# Tags for Hugging Face model paths
14	BNB_QUANTIZED_TAG = "bnb-4bit"
15	UNSLOTH_DYNAMIC_QUANT_TAG = "unsloth" + "-" + BNB_QUANTIZED_TAG
16	GGUF_TAG = "GGUF"
17	BF16_TAG = "bf16"
18	
19	QUANT_TAG_MAP = {
20	    QuantType.BNB: BNB_QUANTIZED_TAG,
21	    QuantType.UNSLOTH: UNSLOTH_DYNAMIC_QUANT_TAG,
22	    QuantType.GGUF: GGUF_TAG,
23	    QuantType.NONE: None,
24	    QuantType.BF16: BF16_TAG,
25	} 
26	
27	# NOTE: models registered with org="unsloth" and QUANT_TYPE.NONE are aliases of QUANT_TYPE.UNSLOTH
28	@dataclass
29	class ModelInfo:
30	    org: str
31	    base_name: str
32	    version: str
33	    size: int
34	    name: str = None  # full model name, constructed from base_name, version, and size unless provided
35	    is_multimodal: bool = False
36	    instruct_tag: str = None
37	    quant_type: QuantType = None
38	    description: str = None
39	
40	    def __post_init__(self):
41	        self.name = self.name or self.construct_model_name(
42	            self.base_name,
43	            self.version,
44	            self.size,
45	            self.quant_type,
46	            self.instruct_tag,
47	        )
48	
49	    @staticmethod
50	    def append_instruct_tag(key: str, instruct_tag: str = None):
51	        if instruct_tag:
52	            key = "-".join([key, instruct_tag])
53	        return key
54	
55	    @staticmethod
56	    def append_quant_type(
57	        key: str, quant_type: QuantType = None
58	    ):
59	        if quant_type != QuantType.NONE:
60	            key = "-".join([key, QUANT_TAG_MAP[quant_type]])
61	        return key
62	
63	    @classmethod
64	    def construct_model_name(cls, base_name, version, size, quant_type, instruct_tag, key=""):
65	        key = cls.append_instruct_tag(key, instruct_tag)
66	        key = cls.append_quant_type(key, quant_type)
67	        return key
68	
69	    @property
70	    def model_path(
71	        self,
72	    ) -> str:
73	        return f"{self.org}/{self.name}"
74	
75	
76	@dataclass
77	class ModelMeta:
78	    org: str
79	    base_name: str
80	    model_version: str
81	    model_info_cls: type[ModelInfo]
82	    model_sizes: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
83	    instruct_tags: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
84	    quant_types: list[QuantType] | dict[str, list[QuantType]] = field(default_factory=list)
85	    is_multimodal: bool = False
86	
87	
88	MODEL_REGISTRY: dict[str, ModelInfo] = {}
89	
90	
91	def register_model(
92	    model_info_cls: ModelInfo,
93	    org: str,
94	    base_name: str,
95	    version: str,
96	    size: int,
97	    instruct_tag: str = None,
98	    quant_type: QuantType = None,
99	    is_multimodal: bool = False,
100	    name: str = None,
101	):
102	    name = name or model_info_cls.construct_model_name(
103	        base_name=base_name,
104	        version=version,
105	        size=size,
106	        quant_type=quant_type,
107	        instruct_tag=instruct_tag,
108	    )
109	    key = f"{org}/{name}"
110	
111	    if key in MODEL_REGISTRY:
112	        raise ValueError(f"Model {key} already registered, current keys: {MODEL_REGISTRY.keys()}")
113	
114	    MODEL_REGISTRY[key] = model_info_cls(
115	        org=org,
116	        base_name=base_name,
117	        version=version,
118	        size=size,
119	        is_multimodal=is_multimodal,
120	        instruct_tag=instruct_tag,
121	        quant_type=quant_type,
122	        name=name,
123	    )
124	
125	
126	def _check_model_info(model_id: str, properties: list[str] = ["lastModified"]):
127	    from huggingface_hub import HfApi
128	    from huggingface_hub import ModelInfo as HfModelInfo
129	    from huggingface_hub.utils import RepositoryNotFoundError
130	
131	    api = HfApi()
132	
133	    try:
134	        model_info: HfModelInfo = api.model_info(model_id, expand=properties)
135	    except Exception as e:
136	        if isinstance(e, RepositoryNotFoundError):
137	            warnings.warn(f"{model_id} not found on Hugging Face")
138	            model_info = None
139	        else:
140	            raise e
141	    return model_info
142	
143	
144	def _register_models(model_meta: ModelMeta, include_original_model: bool = False):
145	    org = model_meta.org
146	    base_name = model_meta.base_name
147	    instruct_tags = model_meta.instruct_tags
148	    model_version = model_meta.model_version
149	    model_sizes = model_meta.model_sizes
150	    is_multimodal = model_meta.is_multimodal
151	    quant_types = model_meta.quant_types
152	    model_info_cls = model_meta.model_info_cls
153	
154	    for size in model_sizes:
155	        for instruct_tag in instruct_tags:
156	            # Handle quant types per model size
157	            if isinstance(quant_types, dict):
158	                _quant_types = quant_types[size]
159	            else:
160	                _quant_types = quant_types
161	            for quant_type in _quant_types:
162	                # NOTE: models registered with org="unsloth" and QUANT_TYPE.NONE are aliases of QUANT_TYPE.UNSLOTH
163	                _org = "unsloth" # unsloth models -- these are all quantized versions of the original model
164	                register_model(
165	                    model_info_cls=model_info_cls,
166	                    org=_org,
167	                    base_name=base_name,
168	                    version=model_version,
169	                    size=size,
170	                    instruct_tag=instruct_tag,
171	                    quant_type=quant_type,
172	                    is_multimodal=is_multimodal,
173	                )
174	            # include original model from releasing organization
175	            if include_original_model:
176	                register_model(
177	                    model_info_cls=model_info_cls,
178	                    org=org,
179	                    base_name=base_name,
180	                    version=model_version,
181	                    size=size,
182	                    instruct_tag=instruct_tag,
183	                    quant_type=QuantType.NONE,
184	                    is_multimodal=is_multimodal,
185	                )

2569	def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
2570	    import inspect
2571	    import types
2572	    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List
2573	
2574	    # And now re add our saving methods!
2575	    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
2576	        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
2577	    else:
2578	        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
2579	    pass
2580	
2581	    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
2582	    signature = signature[1:]
2583	    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
2584	    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")
2585	
2586	    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
2587	    """
2588	    {docs}
2589	    """
2590	    arguments = dict(locals())
2591	    del arguments["self"]
2592	    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
2593	        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
2594	        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
2595	    elif "tags" in arguments:
2596	        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
2597	    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
2598	        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

205	def unsloth_save_model(
206	    model,
207	    tokenizer,
208	    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
209	    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
210	    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
211	    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,

213	    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
214	    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,

227	    tags                 : List[str] = None,
228	
229	    # Our functions
230	    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
231	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
232	):
233	    if token is None: token = get_token()
234	
235	    if commit_message is None: commit_message = ""
236	    if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
237	        commit_message += " (Trained with Unsloth)"
238	    commit_message = commit_message.lstrip()
239	
240	    if commit_description is None:
241	        commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
242	    elif "Unsloth 2x faster" not in commit_description:
243	        commit_description += " (Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
244	    pass
245	
246	    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
247	        raise RuntimeError(
248	            "Unsloth: Merging into 4bit will cause your model to lose accuracy if you plan\n"\
249	            "to merge to GGUF or others later on. I suggest you to do this as a final step\n"\
250	            "if you're planning to do multiple saves.\n"\
251	            "If you are certain, change `save_method` to `merged_4bit_forced`."
252	        )
253	    elif save_method == "merged_4bit_forced":
254	        save_method = "merged_4bit"
255	    pass
256	
257	    save_pretrained_settings = dict(locals())
258	    for deletion in ("model", "tokenizer", "save_method", "temporary_location", "maximum_memory_usage"):
259	        del save_pretrained_settings[deletion]
260	    pass
261	
262	    # First check for a token!
263	    if push_to_hub:
264	        from huggingface_hub import whoami
265	        try:
266	            username = whoami(token = token)["name"]
267	        except:
268	            raise RuntimeError(
269	                "Unsloth: Please supply a token!\n"\
270	                "Go to https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens"
271	            )
272	        pass
273	    pass
274	
275	    assert(maximum_memory_usage > 0 and maximum_memory_usage <= 0.95)
276	
277	    # Clean memory up first
278	    for _ in range(3):
279	        torch.cuda.empty_cache()
280	        gc.collect()
281	    pass
282	
283	    save_method = save_method.lower().replace(" ", "_")

285	        raise RuntimeError(
286	            "Unsloth: You must select one of 3 options when saving models:\n"\
287	            '"lora"         ==> This is the fastest and easiet. Just saves LoRA modules.\n'\
288	            '"merged_16bit" ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to float16. Needed for llama.cpp / GGUF.\n'\

294	
295	        print("Unsloth: Merging 4bit and LoRA weights to 4bit...")

301	        pass

952	def save_to_gguf(
953	    model_type           : str,
954	    model_dtype          : str,
955	    is_sentencepiece     : bool = False,
956	    model_directory      : str = "unsloth_finetuned_model",
957	    quantization_method  = "fast_quantized", # Can be a list of options! ["q4_k_m", "q8_0", "q5_k_m"]
958	    first_conversion     : str = None,
959	    _run_installer = None, # Non blocking install of llama.cpp
960	):
961	    # logger.warning(
962	    #     "NOTICE: llama.cpp GGUF conversion is currently unstable, since llama.cpp is\n"\
963	    #     "undergoing some major bug fixes as at 5th of May 2024. This is not an Unsloth issue.\n"\
964	    #     "Please be patient - GGUF saving should still work, but might not work as well."
965	    # )
966	    assert(model_dtype == "float16" or model_dtype == "bfloat16")
967	    model_dtype = "f16" if model_dtype == "float16" else "bf16"
968	
969	    # Convert quantization_method to list
970	    if   isinstance(quantization_method, list):  pass
971	    elif isinstance(quantization_method, str):   quantization_method = [ quantization_method, ]
972	    elif isinstance(quantization_method, tuple): quantization_method = list(quantization_method)
973	    else:
974	        raise TypeError("Unsloth: quantization_method can only be a string or a list of strings")
975	    pass
976	
977	    # Check if bfloat16 is supported
978	    if model_dtype == "bf16" and not torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported():
979	        logger.warning(
980	            "Unsloth: Cannot convert to bf16 GGUF since your computer doesn't support it.\n"\
981	            "We shall switch instead to f16."
982	        )
983	        model_dtype = "f16"
984	    pass
985	
986	    # Check first_conversion as well
987	    if first_conversion is None:
988	        first_conversion = model_dtype
989	    pass
990	
991	    # Check I quants
992	    for quant_method in quantization_method:
993	        if quant_method.startswith("iq2"):
994	            raise RuntimeError("Unsloth: Currently iq2 type quantizations aren't supported yet - sorry!")
995	    pass
996	
997	    # Careful convert.py is only for Llama / Mistral based archs
998	    use_fast_convert = False
999	    if not is_sentencepiece:      use_fast_convert = False # Llama-3
1000	    elif model_type == "llama":   use_fast_convert = True
1001	    elif model_type == "mistral": use_fast_convert = True
1002	    pass
1003	    logger.warning_once(f"Unsloth: Converting {model_type} model. Can use fast conversion = {use_fast_convert}.")
1004	
1005	    # Map quant methods
1006	    new_quantization_method = []
1007	    for quant_method in quantization_method:
1008	        if   quant_method == "not_quantized":  quant_method = model_dtype
1009	        elif quant_method == "fast_quantized": quant_method = "q8_0"
1010	        elif quant_method == "quantized":      quant_method = "q4_k_m"
1011	        elif quant_method is None:             quant_method = "q8_0"
1012	
1013	        # Check if wrong method
1014	        if quant_method not in ALLOWED_QUANTS.keys():
1015	            error = f"Unsloth: Quant method = [{quant_method}] not supported. Choose from below:\n"
1016	            for key, value in ALLOWED_QUANTS.items():
1017	                error += f"[{key}] => {value}\n"
1018	            raise RuntimeError(error)
1019	        pass
1020	
1021	        new_quantization_method.append(quant_method)
1022	    pass
1023	    quantization_method = new_quantization_method
1024	
1025	    print_info = \
1026	        f"==((====))==  Unsloth: Conversion from QLoRA to GGUF information\n"\
1027	        f"   {chr(92)}{chr(92)}   /|    [0] Installing llama.cpp might take 3 minutes.\n"\
1028	        f"O^O/ {chr(92)}_/ {chr(92)}    [1] Converting HF to GGUF 16bits might take 3 minutes.\n"\
1029	        f"{chr(92)}        /    [2] Converting GGUF 16bits to {quantization_method} might take 10 minutes each.\n"\
1030	        f' "-____-"     In total, you will have to wait at least 16 minutes.\n'
1031	    print(print_info)
1032	
1033	    # Check first_conversion format
1034	    if   first_conversion == "f16"  : pass
1035	    elif first_conversion == "bf16" : pass
1036	    elif first_conversion == "f32"  : pass
1037	    elif first_conversion == "q8_0" : pass
1038	    else:
1039	        raise RuntimeError(
1040	            f"Unsloth: `first_conversion` can only be one of ['f16', 'bf16', 'f32', 'q8_0'] and not `{first_conversion}`."
1041	        )
1042	    pass
1043	
1044	    # Determine whether the system already has llama.cpp installed and the scripts are executable
1045	    quantize_location = get_executable(["llama-quantize", "quantize", "llama-quantize.exe", "quantize.exe"])
1046	    convert_location  = get_executable(["convert-hf-to-gguf.py", "convert_hf_to_gguf.py"])
1047	
1048	    error = 0
1049	    if quantize_location is not None and convert_location is not None:
1050	        print("Unsloth: llama.cpp found in the system. We shall skip installation.")
1051	    else:
1052	        print("Unsloth: Installing llama.cpp. This might take 3 minutes...")
1053	        if _run_installer is not None:
1054	            _run_installer, IS_CMAKE = _run_installer
1055	
1056	            error = _run_installer.wait()
1057	            # Check if successful
1058	            if error != 0:
1059	                print(f"Unsloth: llama.cpp error code = {error}.")
1060	                install_llama_cpp_old(-10)
1061	            pass
1062	
1063	            if IS_CMAKE:
1064	                # CMAKE needs to do some extra steps
1065	                print("Unsloth: CMAKE detected. Finalizing some steps for installation.")
1066	
1067	                check = os.system("cp llama.cpp/build/bin/llama-* llama.cpp")
1068	                if check != 0: raise RuntimeError("Failed compiling llama.cpp. Please report this ASAP!")
1069	                check = os.system("rm -rf llama.cpp/build")
1070	                if check != 0: raise RuntimeError("Failed compiling llama.cpp. Please report this ASAP!")
1071	            pass
1072	        else:
1073	            error = 0
1074	            install_llama_cpp_blocking()
1075	        pass
1076	
1077	        # Careful llama.cpp/quantize changed to llama.cpp/llama-quantize
1078	        # and llama.cpp/main changed to llama.cpp/llama-cli
1079	        # See https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/pull/7809
1080	        quantize_location = None
1081	        if os.path.exists("llama.cpp/quantize.exe"):
1082	            quantize_location = "llama.cpp/quantize.exe"
1083	        elif os.path.exists("llama.cpp/quantize"):
1084	            quantize_location = "llama.cpp/quantize"
1085	        elif os.path.exists("llama.cpp/llama-quantize.exe"):
1086	            quantize_location = "llama.cpp/llama-quantize.exe"
1087	        elif os.path.exists("llama.cpp/llama-quantize"):
1088	            quantize_location = "llama.cpp/llama-quantize"
1089	        elif os.path.exists("llama.cpp/build/bin/llama-quantize"):
1090	            quantize_location = "llama.cpp/build/bin/llama-quantize"
1091	        elif os.path.exists("llama.cpp/build/bin/quantize"):
1092	            quantize_location = "llama.cpp/build/bin/quantize"
1093	        else:
1094	            raise RuntimeError(
1095	                "Unsloth: The file 'llama.cpp/llama-quantize' or `llama.cpp/quantize` does not exist.\n"\
1096	                "We've also double checked the building directory under 'llama.cpp/build/bin/'.\n"\
1097	                "But we expect this file to exist! Check if the file exists under llama.cpp and investigate the building process of llama.cpp (make/cmake)!"
1098	            )
1099	        pass
1100

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias
187	pass

260	def _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X: torch.Tensor, proj: torch.nn.Module) -> torch.Tensor:
261	    """
262	    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.
263	    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
264	    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.
265	    """
266	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
267	    activation_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "activation_fake_quantizer", None)
268	    if activation_fake_quantizer is not None:
269	        X = activation_fake_quantizer(X)
270	    return X
271	pass

275	if DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu" and HAS_XPU_STREAM:
276	    @torch.inference_mode
277	    def fast_dequantize(W, quant_state = None, out = None, use_global_buffer = False):
278	        # TODO: After adding XPU BNB support, check this function
279	        if quant_state is None: return W
280	        if type(quant_state) is not list:
281	            # New quant_state as a class
282	            # https://github.com/TimDettmers/bitsandbytes/pull/763/files
283	            absmax     = quant_state.absmax
284	            shape      = quant_state.shape
285	            dtype      = quant_state.dtype
286	            blocksize  = quant_state.blocksize
287	            offset     = quant_state.offset
288	            state2     = quant_state.state2
289	            absmax2    = state2.absmax
290	            code2      = state2.code
291	            blocksize2 = state2.blocksize
292	        else:
293	            # Old quant_state as a list of lists
294	            absmax, shape, dtype, blocksize, compressed_stats, _, _ = quant_state
295	            offset, state2 = compressed_stats
296	            absmax2, code2, blocksize2, _, _, _, _ = state2
297	        pass
298	        global XPU_STREAMS
299	        device = W.device
300	        device_index = device.index
301	        XPU_STREAM = XPU_STREAMS[device_index]
302	
303	        n_elements_absmax = absmax.numel()
304	        # Create weight matrix
305	        if use_global_buffer:
306	
307	            # Use same buffers for faster inference
308	            size = shape[0]*shape[1]
309	            global WEIGHT_BUFFERS
310	            global ABSMAX_BUFFERS
311	            WEIGHT_BUFFER = WEIGHT_BUFFERS[device_index]
312	            ABSMAX_BUFFER = ABSMAX_BUFFERS[device_index]
313	            if WEIGHT_BUFFER is None:
314	                WEIGHT_BUFFERS[device_index] = WEIGHT_BUFFER = torch_empty(size, dtype = dtype, device = device, requires_grad = False)

2357	    model,
2358	    tokenizer,
2359	    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike] = "unsloth_finetuned_merge",

2370

2374	    private              : Optional[bool] = None,

2413	def unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged(
2414	    self,
2415	    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
2416	    tokenizer            = None,
2417	    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
2418	    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
2419	    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
2420	    is_main_process      : bool = True,
2421	    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
2422	    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
2423	    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
2424	    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
2425	    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
2426	    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
2427	    tags                 : List[str] = None,
2428	    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
2429	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
2430	):
2431	    """
2432	        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
2433	        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.
2434	
2435	        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
2436	        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
2437	        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
2438	        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
2439	    """
2440	    if tokenizer is None:
2441	        logger.warning_once(
2442	            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
2443	            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.save_pretrained(...)`"
2444	        )
2445	    pass
2446

86	def _get_new_mapper():
87	    try:
88	        import requests
89	        new_mapper = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/unslothai/unsloth/main/unsloth/models/mapper.py"
90	        with requests.get(new_mapper, timeout = 3) as new_mapper: new_mapper = new_mapper.text
91	        new_mapper = new_mapper[new_mapper.find("__INT_TO_FLOAT_MAPPER"):]
92	        new_mapper = new_mapper\
93	            .replace("INT_TO_FLOAT_MAPPER",  "NEW_INT_TO_FLOAT_MAPPER")\
94	            .replace("FLOAT_TO_INT_MAPPER",  "NEW_FLOAT_TO_INT_MAPPER")\
95	            .replace("MAP_TO_UNSLOTH_16bit", "NEW_MAP_TO_UNSLOTH_16bit")
96	
97	        exec(new_mapper, globals())
98	        return NEW_INT_TO_FLOAT_MAPPER, NEW_FLOAT_TO_INT_MAPPER, NEW_MAP_TO_UNSLOTH_16bit
99	    except:
100	        return {}, {}, {}
101	    pass

54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55

58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59

61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)

88	                              X, e, g)

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a summary document with all the key findings:

## Comprehensive Exploration Report: Unsloth Save/Export Codebase

### 1. **Top-Level Directory Structure**

```
/unsloth/
├── __init__.py                 # Main entry point, exports save.py and models
├── save.py                     # PRIMARY: 2700+ lines with all save/export functionality
├── chat_templates.py           # Chat template utilities
├── tokenizer_utils.py          # Tokenizer utilities
├── trainer.py                  # Trainer integration
├── kernels/                    # LoRA kernel operations
│   ├── utils.py               # get_lora_parameters_bias() - extracts LoRA A,B matrices
│   ├── fast_lora.py           # LoRA forward/backward computation
│   └── __init__.py
├── models/                     # Model-specific implementations
│   ├── loader_utils.py        # get_model_name() for model mapping
│   ├── llama.py               # Llama architecture (131KB)
│   ├── _utils.py              # Shared utilities (68KB)
│   ├── vision.py              # Vision model support
│   └── [other models]
├── registry/                   # Model registry system
│   ├── registry.py            # ModelInfo, register_model(), MODEL_REGISTRY
│   └── [model registrations]
└── utils/                      # Utility functions
```

---

### 2. **Key Save/Export Modules**

#### **A. Main Save Module: `/unsloth/save.py` (105KB)**

**__all__ exports:**
```python
"print_quantization_methods",
"unsloth_save_model",
"save_to_gguf",
"patch_saving_functions",
"create_huggingface_repo",
```

**Core Functions:**

1. **`unsloth_save_model(model, tokenizer, save_directory, save_method, ...)`** (Line 205-760)
   - Main save orchestrator function
   - **save_method options:** `"lora"`, `"merged_16bit"`, `"merged_4bit"`
   - Handles memory management, state dict creation, and saving
   - Supports push_to_hub for Hugging Face
   - **Key logic flow:**
     - Validates save_method
     - For LoRA: calls `model.save_pretrained()`
     - For merged: builds custom state_dict by:
       - Iterating through `internal_model.model.layers`
       - Calling `_merge_lora()` for each weight layer
       - Merging LoRA A and B matrices into base weights
       - Saving with tokenizer configuration

2. **`_merge_lora(layer, name)`** (Line 159-187)
   - **Extracts:** LoRA A, B matrices and scaling factor from layer
   - Calls `get_lora_parameters_bias()` from kernels
   - **Merge operation:** `W.addmm_(A.t(), B.t(), alpha=s)` (line 176)
     - Uses PyTorch's efficient `addmm_` (add matrix multiply in-place)
     - Formula: `W = W + (A.T @ B.T) * s`
   - Handles quantized and unquantized layers

3. **`save_to_gguf(model_type, model_dtype, model_directory, quantization_method, ...)`** (Line 952-1320)
   - Converts 4bit merged model to GGUF format
   - Installs llama.cpp (non-blocking)
   - Supports quantization methods: q8_0, q4_k_m, q5_k_m, etc.
   - Uses separate conversion process

4. **`unsloth_save_pretrained_merged(save_directory, tokenizer, save_method, ...)`** (Line 1321-1361)
   - Instance method wrapper (added to model via `patch_saving_functions`)
   - Calls `unsloth_save_model()` with `merged_16bit` by default

5. **`unsloth_push_to_hub_merged(repo_id, tokenizer, save_method, ...)`** (Line 1364-1407)
   - Instance method wrapper
   - Calls `unsloth_save_model()` with `push_to_hub=True`

6. **`unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(save_directory, tokenizer, quantization_method, ...)`** (Line 1768-1950)
   - Saves model as GGUF format
   - Merges 4bit→16bit, then 16bit→GGUF
   - Supports quantization method options

7. **`unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf(repo_id, tokenizer, quantization_method, ...)`** (Line 1951-2128)
   - Saves and pushes GGUF to Hugging Face Hub

8. **`save_lora_to_custom_dir(model, tokenizer, save_directory)`** (Line 2129-2140)
   - Saves only LoRA adapters (not merged weights)

9. **`unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub(...)`** (Line 2143-2210)
   - Converts LoRA adapters to GGML format
   - Pushes to HF Hub

10. **`unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally(...)`** (Line 2211-2275)
    - Converts LoRA to GGML format locally

11. **`patch_saving_functions(model, vision=False)`** (Line 2569-2678)
    - **REGISTRATION MECHANISM**: Dynamically attaches methods to model instance
    - Uses Python's `types.MethodType()` to bind functions
    - **Attached methods:**
      ```python
      model.push_to_hub_merged     = unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged
      model.save_pretrained_merged = unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged
      model.push_to_hub_gguf       = unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf
      model.save_pretrained_gguf   = unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf
      model.save_pretrained_torchao = unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao
      model.push_to_hub_ggml       = unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub
      model.save_pretrained_ggml   = unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally
      ```
    - Also patches `model.push_to_hub()` wrapper to add "unsloth" tags

---

### 3. **LoRA Adapter Handling**

#### **A. LoRA A and B Matrix Extraction**

**Location:** `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (Line 235-257)

**Function: `get_lora_parameters_bias(proj)`**

```python
def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    # Returns if adapters disabled/merged
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    
    # Get active adapter name
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    # Extract and return A, B matrices
    return (
        W,                              # Weight matrix
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None), # Quantization state
        proj.lora_A[adapter].weight,    # LoRA A matrix
        proj.lora_B[adapter].weight,    # LoRA B matrix
        proj.scaling[adapter],          # LoRA scaling factor
        base_layer.bias,                # Bias term
    )
```

**Key Points:**
- LoRA matrices stored in `proj.lora_A[adapter_name].weight` and `proj.lora_B[adapter_name].weight`
- Scaling factor stored in `proj.scaling[adapter_name]`
- Adapter name retrieved from `active_adapters` or defaults to `"default"`
- Used by `_merge_lora()` in save.py

#### **B. LoRA Merge Formula**

**In save.py, Line 176:**
```python
W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha=s)
```

**Explanation:**
- `addmm_`: in-place add matrix multiply
- Equivalent to: `W = W + s * (A.T @ B.T)`
- A: shape `(r, in_features)`, B: shape `(r, out_features)`
- Result: LoRA update added to weight matrix

---

### 4. **Method Registration System**

#### **A. How Methods Are Registered**

**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py`, Line 2569-2678 in `patch_saving_functions()`

**Pattern: Dynamic Method Binding Using `types.MethodType`**

```python
import types

# Save method wrapped as instance method
model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(
    unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,  # Function
    model                                     # Instance to bind to
)
```

**Registration Flow:**
1. Called from model loader (implicitly when loading model with Unsloth)
2. Takes model instance as parameter
3. Uses `types.MethodType()` to create bound methods
4. Attaches to model instance (not class)
5. Can be called as `model.save_pretrained_merged(...)`

**Registered Methods:**
- `model.save_pretrained_merged()`
- `model.push_to_hub_merged()`
- `model.save_pretrained_gguf()`
- `model.push_to_hub_gguf()`
- `model.save_pretrained_ggml()`
- `model.push_to_hub_ggml()`
- `model.save_pretrained_torchao()`
- `model.push_to_hub()` (wrapper)

---

### 5. **Generic Save Functions (For Non-Llama Models)**

**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py`, Line 2356-2500

**Key Functions:**

1. **`unsloth_generic_save(...)`** (Line 2356-2410)
   - Generic model save for architectures without custom merge logic
   - Calls `merge_and_overwrite_lora()` from `unsloth_zoo.saving_utils`
   - **Note:** Implementation delegated to external library

2. **`unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged(...)`** (Line 2413-2453)
   - Generic wrapper method

3. **`unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged(...)`** (Line 2456-2499)
   - Generic push wrapper

---

### 6. **External Dependency for Generic Models**

**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py`, Line 2265-2273

```python
from .models.loader_utils import get_model_name
from unsloth_zoo.saving_utils import (
    merge_and_overwrite_lora,      # Generic LoRA merging
    prepare_saving,                 # Save preparation
)
from unsloth_zoo.llama_cpp import (
    install_llama_cpp,
    convert_to_gguf as _convert_to_gguf,
)
```

**Key Dependencies:**
- `merge_and_overwrite_lora()`: Generic LoRA merge implementation (from unsloth_zoo)
- `prepare_saving()`: Pre-save preparation
- llama.cpp integration for GGUF conversion

---

### 7. **Model Registry System**

**Location:** `/unsloth/registry/registry.py`

**Key Classes:**

```python
class QuantType(Enum):
    BNB = "bnb"
    UNSLOTH = "unsloth"
    GGUF = "GGUF"
    NONE = "none"
    BF16 = "bf16"

@dataclass
class ModelInfo:
    org: str
    base_name: str
    version: str
    size: int
    quant_type: QuantType = None
    is_multimodal: bool = False

MODEL_REGISTRY: dict[str, ModelInfo] = {}

def register_model(model_info_cls, org, base_name, version, size, ...):
    # Registers model in global registry
```

**Purpose:** Tracks available models, quantization types, and metadata

---

### 8. **Key Classes and Their Relationships**

```
Model Instance (e.g., PeftModelForCausalLM)
│
├─ model.config                    # Model configuration
├─ model.model.layers[i]           # Individual transformer layers
│  └─ layer.self_attn.q_proj      # Attention projection with LoRA
│     ├─ .lora_A["default"]       # LoRA A matrix (shape: r × in_features)
│     ├─ .lora_B["default"]       # LoRA B matrix (shape: r × out_features)
│     ├─ .scaling["default"]       # LoRA scaling factor
│     └─ .weight                   # Base weight matrix
│
└─ Registered Methods (via patch_saving_functions):
   ├─ .save_pretrained_merged()
   ├─ .save_pretrained_gguf()
   ├─ .save_pretrained_ggml()
   └─ .save_pretrained_torchao()
```

---

### 9. **Important Constants and Configurations**

**In save.py:**

```python
# Weight layers for Llama-like models (Line 67-70)
LLAMA_WEIGHTS = (
    "self_attn.q_proj", "self_attn.k_proj", "self_attn.v_proj", "self_attn.o_proj",
    "mlp.gate_proj", "mlp.up_proj", "mlp.down_proj",
)

# Layer norms to preserve (Line 71-75)
LLAMA_LAYERNORMS = (
    "input_layernorm", "post_attention_layernorm",
    "pre_feedforward_layernorm", "post_feedforward_layernorm",
    "self_attn.q_norm", "self_attn.k_norm",
)

# GGUF quantization methods (Line 79-100)
ALLOWED_QUANTS = {
    "not_quantized": "...",
    "fast_quantized": "q8_0",
    "quantized": "q4_k_m",
    # ... many more options
}
```

---

### 10. **Save Method Flow Diagram**

```
User calls: model.save_pretrained_merged(path, save_method="merged_16bit")
    ↓
patch_saving_functions() attached method
    ↓
unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()  [wrapper]
    ↓
unsloth_generic_save()
    ↓
merge_and_overwrite_lora()  [from unsloth_zoo]
    ↓
Save to HuggingFace format

OR for Llama models:

User calls: model.save_pretrained_merged(path, save_method="merged_16bit")
    ↓
patch_saving_functions() attached method
    ↓
unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()  [wrapper]
    ↓
unsloth_save_model()  [Main function]
    ↓
For each layer:
    - _merge_lora(layer)
      - get_lora_parameters_bias()
      - Extract A, B matrices
      - Compute: W = W + (A.T @ B.T) * s
    - Add merged weight to state_dict
    ↓
model.save_pretrained(state_dict=custom_state_dict)
    ↓
Save to disk/HuggingFace
```

---

### 11. **Test Examples**

**Location:** `/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py`

**Key Test Functions:**
- `test_save_merged_16bit()`: Tests merged 16-bit saving
- `test_save_merged_4bit()`: Tests merged 4-bit saving
- `test_save_torchao()`: Tests TorchAO quantization

**Usage Pattern:**
```python
model.save_pretrained_merged(
    save_path,
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    save_method="merged_16bit"
)
```

---

### 12. **File Paths Summary**

| Component | File Path |
|-----------|-----------|
| Main save/export | `/unsloth/save.py` |
| LoRA parameter extraction | `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` |
| LoRA forward/backward | `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` |
| Model registry | `/unsloth/registry/registry.py` |
| Model loading utils | `/unsloth/models/loader_utils.py` |
| Tests | `/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py` |
| CLI entry | `/unsloth-cli.py` |

---

### Summary

The unsloth codebase uses a **dynamic method binding system** to register save/export methods on model instances at runtime. The core save functionality resides in `/unsloth/save.py`, with LoRA-specific operations handled in `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py`. The system supports multiple save methods (LoRA only, merged 16-bit, merged 4-bit, GGUF, GGML, TorchAO) with different memory/speed tradeoffs. Generic model architectures delegate to `unsloth_zoo.saving_utils`, while Llama-like models use custom optimized merge logic.

## Comprehensive Exploration Report: Unsloth Save/Export Codebase

### 1. **Top-Level Directory Structure**

```
/unsloth/
├── __init__.py                 # Main entry point, exports save.py and models
├── save.py                     # PRIMARY: 2700+ lines with all save/export functionality
├── chat_templates.py           # Chat template utilities
├── tokenizer_utils.py          # Tokenizer utilities
├── trainer.py                  # Trainer integration
├── kernels/                    # LoRA kernel operations
│   ├── utils.py               # get_lora_parameters_bias() - extracts LoRA A,B matrices
│   ├── fast_lora.py           # LoRA forward/backward computation
│   └── __init__.py
├── models/                     # Model-specific implementations
│   ├── loader_utils.py        # get_model_name() for model mapping
│   ├── llama.py               # Llama architecture (131KB)
│   ├── _utils.py              # Shared utilities (68KB)
│   ├── vision.py              # Vision model support
│   └── [other models]
├── registry/                   # Model registry system
│   ├── registry.py            # ModelInfo, register_model(), MODEL_REGISTRY
│   └── [model registrations]
└── utils/                      # Utility functions
```

---

### 2. **Key Save/Export Modules**

#### **A. Main Save Module: `/unsloth/save.py` (105KB)**

**__all__ exports:**
```python
"print_quantization_methods",
"unsloth_save_model",
"save_to_gguf",
"patch_saving_functions",
"create_huggingface_repo",
```

**Core Functions:**

1. **`unsloth_save_model(model, tokenizer, save_directory, save_method, ...)`** (Line 205-760)
   - Main save orchestrator function
   - **save_method options:** `"lora"`, `"merged_16bit"`, `"merged_4bit"`
   - Handles memory management, state dict creation, and saving
   - Supports push_to_hub for Hugging Face
   - **Key logic flow:**
     - Validates save_method
     - For LoRA: calls `model.save_pretrained()`
     - For merged: builds custom state_dict by:
       - Iterating through `internal_model.model.layers`
       - Calling `_merge_lora()` for each weight layer
       - Merging LoRA A and B matrices into base weights
       - Saving with tokenizer configuration

2. **`_merge_lora(layer, name)`** (Line 159-187)
   - **Extracts:** LoRA A, B matrices and scaling factor from layer
   - Calls `get_lora_parameters_bias()` from kernels
   - **Merge operation:** `W.addmm_(A.t(), B.t(), alpha=s)` (line 176)
     - Uses PyTorch's efficient `addmm_` (add matrix multiply in-place)
     - Formula: `W = W + (A.T @ B.T) * s`
   - Handles quantized and unquantized layers

3. **`save_to_gguf(model_type, model_dtype, model_directory, quantization_method, ...)`** (Line 952-1320)
   - Converts 4bit merged model to GGUF format
   - Installs llama.cpp (non-blocking)
   - Supports quantization methods: q8_0, q4_k_m, q5_k_m, etc.
   - Uses separate conversion process

4. **`unsloth_save_pretrained_merged(save_directory, tokenizer, save_method, ...)`** (Line 1321-1361)
   - Instance method wrapper (added to model via `patch_saving_functions`)
   - Calls `unsloth_save_model()` with `merged_16bit` by default

5. **`unsloth_push_to_hub_merged(repo_id, tokenizer, save_method, ...)`** (Line 1364-1407)
   - Instance method wrapper
   - Calls `unsloth_save_model()` with `push_to_hub=True`

6. **`unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(save_directory, tokenizer, quantization_method, ...)`** (Line 1768-1950)
   - Saves model as GGUF format
   - Merges 4bit→16bit, then 16bit→GGUF
   - Supports quantization method options

7. **`unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf(repo_id, tokenizer, quantization_method, ...)`** (Line 1951-2128)
   - Saves and pushes GGUF to Hugging Face Hub

8. **`save_lora_to_custom_dir(model, tokenizer, save_directory)`** (Line 2129-2140)
   - Saves only LoRA adapters (not merged weights)

9. **`unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub(...)`** (Line 2143-2210)
   - Converts LoRA adapters to GGML format
   - Pushes to HF Hub

10. **`unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally(...)`** (Line 2211-2275)
    - Converts LoRA to GGML format locally

11. **`patch_saving_functions(model, vision=False)`** (Line 2569-2678)
    - **REGISTRATION MECHANISM**: Dynamically attaches methods to model instance
    - Uses Python's `types.MethodType()` to bind functions
    - **Attached methods:**
      ```python
      model.push_to_hub_merged     = unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged
      model.save_pretrained_merged = unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged
      model.push_to_hub_gguf       = unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf
      model.save_pretrained_gguf   = unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf
      model.save_pretrained_torchao = unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao
      model.push_to_hub_ggml       = unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub
      model.save_pretrained_ggml   = unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally
      ```
    - Also patches `model.push_to_hub()` wrapper to add "unsloth" tags

---

### 3. **LoRA Adapter Handling**

#### **A. LoRA A and B Matrix Extraction**

**Location:** `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (Line 235-257)

**Function: `get_lora_parameters_bias(proj)`**

```python
def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    # Returns if adapters disabled/merged
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    
    # Get active adapter name
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    # Extract and return A, B matrices
    return (
        W,                              # Weight matrix
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None), # Quantization state
        proj.lora_A[adapter].weight,    # LoRA A matrix
        proj.lora_B[adapter].weight,    # LoRA B matrix
        proj.scaling[adapter],          # LoRA scaling factor
        base_layer.bias,                # Bias term
    )
```

**Key Points:**
- LoRA matrices stored in `proj.lora_A[adapter_name].weight` and `proj.lora_B[adapter_name].weight`
- Scaling factor stored in `proj.scaling[adapter_name]`
- Adapter name retrieved from `active_adapters` or defaults to `"default"`
- Used by `_merge_lora()` in save.py

#### **B. LoRA Merge Formula**

**In save.py, Line 176:**
```python
W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha=s)
```

**Explanation:**
- `addmm_`: in-place add matrix multiply
- Equivalent to: `W = W + s * (A.T @ B.T)`
- A: shape `(r, in_features)`, B: shape `(r, out_features)`
- Result: LoRA update added to weight matrix

---

### 4. **Method Registration System**

#### **A. How Methods Are Registered**

**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py`, Line 2569-2678 in `patch_saving_functions()`

**Pattern: Dynamic Method Binding Using `types.MethodType`**

```python
import types

# Save method wrapped as instance method
model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(
    unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,  # Function
    model                                     # Instance to bind to
)
```

**Registration Flow:**
1. Called from model loader (implicitly when loading model with Unsloth)
2. Takes model instance as parameter
3. Uses `types.MethodType()` to create bound methods
4. Attaches to model instance (not class)
5. Can be called as `model.save_pretrained_merged(...)`

**Registered Methods:**
- `model.save_pretrained_merged()`
- `model.push_to_hub_merged()`
- `model.save_pretrained_gguf()`
- `model.push_to_hub_gguf()`
- `model.save_pretrained_ggml()`
- `model.push_to_hub_ggml()`
- `model.save_pretrained_torchao()`
- `model.push_to_hub()` (wrapper)

---

### 5. **Generic Save Functions (For Non-Llama Models)**

**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py`, Line 2356-2500

**Key Functions:**

1. **`unsloth_generic_save(...)`** (Line 2356-2410)
   - Generic model save for architectures without custom merge logic
   - Calls `merge_and_overwrite_lora()` from `unsloth_zoo.saving_utils`
   - **Note:** Implementation delegated to external library

2. **`unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged(...)`** (Line 2413-2453)
   - Generic wrapper method

3. **`unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged(...)`** (Line 2456-2499)
   - Generic push wrapper

---

### 6. **External Dependency for Generic Models**

**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py`, Line 2265-2273

```python
from .models.loader_utils import get_model_name
from unsloth_zoo.saving_utils import (
    merge_and_overwrite_lora,      # Generic LoRA merging
    prepare_saving,                 # Save preparation
)
from unsloth_zoo.llama_cpp import (
    install_llama_cpp,
    convert_to_gguf as _convert_to_gguf,
)
```

**Key Dependencies:**
- `merge_and_overwrite_lora()`: Generic LoRA merge implementation (from unsloth_zoo)
- `prepare_saving()`: Pre-save preparation
- llama.cpp integration for GGUF conversion

---

### 7. **Model Registry System**

**Location:** `/unsloth/registry/registry.py`

**Key Classes:**

```python
class QuantType(Enum):
    BNB = "bnb"
    UNSLOTH = "unsloth"
    GGUF = "GGUF"
    NONE = "none"
    BF16 = "bf16"

@dataclass
class ModelInfo:
    org: str
    base_name: str
    version: str
    size: int
    quant_type: QuantType = None
    is_multimodal: bool = False

MODEL_REGISTRY: dict[str, ModelInfo] = {}

def register_model(model_info_cls, org, base_name, version, size, ...):
    # Registers model in global registry
```

**Purpose:** Tracks available models, quantization types, and metadata

---

### 8. **Key Classes and Their Relationships**

```
Model Instance (e.g., PeftModelForCausalLM)
│
├─ model.config                    # Model configuration
├─ model.model.layers[i]           # Individual transformer layers
│  └─ layer.self_attn.q_proj      # Attention projection with LoRA
│     ├─ .lora_A["default"]       # LoRA A matrix (shape: r × in_features)
│     ├─ .lora_B["default"]       # LoRA B matrix (shape: r × out_features)
│     ├─ .scaling["default"]       # LoRA scaling factor
│     └─ .weight                   # Base weight matrix
│
└─ Registered Methods (via patch_saving_functions):
   ├─ .save_pretrained_merged()
   ├─ .save_pretrained_gguf()
   ├─ .save_pretrained_ggml()
   └─ .save_pretrained_torchao()
```

---

### 9. **Important Constants and Configurations**

**In save.py:**

```python
# Weight layers for Llama-like models (Line 67-70)
LLAMA_WEIGHTS = (
    "self_attn.q_proj", "self_attn.k_proj", "self_attn.v_proj", "self_attn.o_proj",
    "mlp.gate_proj", "mlp.up_proj", "mlp.down_proj",
)

# Layer norms to preserve (Line 71-75)
LLAMA_LAYERNORMS = (
    "input_layernorm", "post_attention_layernorm",
    "pre_feedforward_layernorm", "post_feedforward_layernorm",
    "self_attn.q_norm", "self_attn.k_norm",
)

# GGUF quantization methods (Line 79-100)
ALLOWED_QUANTS = {
    "not_quantized": "...",
    "fast_quantized": "q8_0",
    "quantized": "q4_k_m",
    # ... many more options
}
```

---

### 10. **Save Method Flow Diagram**

```
User calls: model.save_pretrained_merged(path, save_method="merged_16bit")
    ↓
patch_saving_functions() attached method
    ↓
unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()  [wrapper]
    ↓
unsloth_generic_save()
    ↓
merge_and_overwrite_lora()  [from unsloth_zoo]
    ↓
Save to HuggingFace format

OR for Llama models:

User calls: model.save_pretrained_merged(path, save_method="merged_16bit")
    ↓
patch_saving_functions() attached method
    ↓
unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()  [wrapper]
    ↓
unsloth_save_model()  [Main function]
    ↓
For each layer:
    - _merge_lora(layer)
      - get_lora_parameters_bias()
      - Extract A, B matrices
      - Compute: W = W + (A.T @ B.T) * s
    - Add merged weight to state_dict
    ↓
model.save_pretrained(state_dict=custom_state_dict)
    ↓
Save to disk/HuggingFace
```

---

### 11. **Test Examples**

**Location:** `/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py`

**Key Test Functions:**
- `test_save_merged_16bit()`: Tests merged 16-bit saving
- `test_save_merged_4bit()`: Tests merged 4-bit saving
- `test_save_torchao()`: Tests TorchAO quantization

**Usage Pattern:**
```python
model.save_pretrained_merged(
    save_path,
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    save_method="merged_16bit"
)
```

---

### 12. **File Paths Summary**

| Component | File Path |
|-----------|-----------|
| Main save/export | `/unsloth/save.py` |
| LoRA parameter extraction | `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` |
| LoRA forward/backward | `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` |
| Model registry | `/unsloth/registry/registry.py` |
| Model loading utils | `/unsloth/models/loader_utils.py` |
| Tests | `/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py` |
| CLI entry | `/unsloth-cli.py` |

---

### Summary

The unsloth codebase uses a **dynamic method binding system** to register save/export methods on model instances at runtime. The core save functionality resides in `/unsloth/save.py`, with LoRA-specific operations handled in `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py`. The system supports multiple save methods (LoRA only, merged 16-bit, merged 4-bit, GGUF, GGML, TorchAO) with different memory/speed tradeoffs. Generic model architectures delegate to `unsloth_zoo.saving_utils`, while Llama-like models use custom optimized merge logic.

def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        proj.lora_A [adapter].weight,
        proj.lora_B [adapter].weight,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
        base_layer.bias,
    )
pass


def _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X: torch.Tensor, proj: torch.nn.Module) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.
    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.
    """
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    activation_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "activation_fake_quantizer", None)
    if activation_fake_quantizer is not None:
        X = activation_fake_quantizer(X)
    return X
pass

def _merge_lora(layer, name):

    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        # W = W.t()

        if A is not None:
            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
            # W += sAB
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        pass
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
pass


def fast_save_pickle(shard, name):
    # Use this if # CPUs is <= 2
    print(f"Unsloth: Saving {name}...")
    torch.save(
        shard,
        name,
        # HIGHEST_PROTOCOL seems to not work with Pytorch!
        # pickle_module   = pickle,
        # pickle_protocol = pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL,
    )
    return
pass

def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

    # And now re add our saving methods!
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    pass

    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    signature = signature[1:]
    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")

    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message

    if "commit_description" in arguments:
        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
        if commit_description is not None:
            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
        else:
            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description

    # Update model tag
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    pass

    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    pass

    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    pass
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())

    original_model = model
    while True:

        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
            pass
        pass

        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
        else: break
    pass

    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model
pass

def unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    quantization_method  : str = "fast_quantized",
    first_conversion     : str = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    tags                 : List[str] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.85,
):
    """
        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 then converted to GGUF / llama.cpp format.

        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
        "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
        "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
        "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
        "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
        "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
        "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
        "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
        "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
        "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization",
        "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
        "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",
        "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        raise ValueError("Unsloth: Saving to GGUF must have a tokenizer.")

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]        = self
    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
    arguments["save_method"] = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["quantization_method"]
    del arguments["first_conversion"]

    # Fix tokenizer adding an extra BOS token at the front
    fix_bos_token, old_chat_template = fix_tokenizer_bos_token(tokenizer)

    # Non blocking install GGUF first
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):

        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            # Kaggle is weird - no blocking installs, and no CUDA?
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda = False)
            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
            python_install.wait()
        pass
    else:
        try:
            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
            makefile = None
        except:
            # Retry by recloning llama.cpp
            if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
                # Kaggle is weird - no blocking installs, and no CUDA?

# Corrected function to save LoRA to a custom directory
def save_lora_to_custom_dir(model, tokenizer, save_directory):
    # Create the custom directory if it doesn't exist
    os.makedirs(save_directory, exist_ok=True)

    # Call the unsloth_save_model function with the custom directory
    unsloth_save_model(
        model,
        tokenizer,
        save_directory=save_directory,
        save_method="lora",
        push_to_hub=False,
    )

# Corrected method within the model class to convert LoRA to GGML and push to Hugging Face Hub
def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub(
    self,
    tokenizer,
    repo_id: str,
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Converted LoRA to GGML with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    token: Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    create_pr: bool = False,
    revision: str = None,
    commit_description: str = "Convert LoRA to GGML format using Unsloth",
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    lora_directory_push = "lora-to-ggml-push"
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, lora_directory_push)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(lora_directory_push, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {lora_directory_push} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {lora_directory_push} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return

    print(f"Unsloth: Conversion completed! Output file: {output_file}")

    print("Unsloth: Uploading GGML file to Hugging Face Hub...")
    username = upload_to_huggingface(
        self, repo_id, token,
        "GGML converted LoRA", "ggml", output_file, None, private,
    )
    link = f"{repo_id.lstrip('/')}"
    print("Unsloth: Done.")
    print(f"Converted LoRA to GGML and uploaded to https://huggingface.co/{link}")
    print("\nThis GGML making function was made by Maheswar. Ping him @Maheswar on the Unsloth Discord or on HuggingFace (@mahiatlinux) if you like this!")

@torch.inference_mode
def unsloth_generic_save(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike] = "unsloth_finetuned_merge",
    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,

    # Push to hub
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : List[str] = None,

    # Our functions
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
):
    if token is None and push_to_hub: token = get_token()

    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
        raise RuntimeError(
            "Unsloth: Merging into 4bit will cause your model to lose accuracy if you plan\n"\
            "to merge to GGUF or others later on. I suggest you to do this as a final step\n"\
            "if you're planning to do multiple saves.\n"\
            "If you are certain, change `save_method` to `merged_4bit_forced`."
        )
    elif save_method == "merged_4bit_forced":
        save_method = "merged_4bit"

    merge_and_overwrite_lora(
        get_model_name,
        model                = model,
        tokenizer            = tokenizer,
        save_directory       = save_directory,
        push_to_hub          = push_to_hub,
        private              = private,
        token                = token,
        save_method          = save_method,
        output_dtype         = None,
        low_disk_space_usage = True,
        use_temp_file        = False,
    )
    return
pass


def unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    tags                 : List[str] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
):
    """
        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.

        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        logger.warning_once(
            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.save_pretrained(...)`"
        )
    pass

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"] = self
    del arguments["self"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass


def unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged(
    self,
    repo_id              : str,
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    token                : Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str, None] = "5GB",
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : Optional[List[str]] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
):
    """
        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.

        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        logger.warning_once(
            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.push_to_hub(...)`"
        )
    pass

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]          = self
    arguments["save_directory"] = repo_id
    arguments["push_to_hub"]    = True
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["repo_id"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass


def unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    torchao_config       = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
):
    """Quantizes the model with torchao and saves a torchao quantized checkpoint

    Args
      `save_directory`: local folder path or huggingface hub ID when `push_to_hub` is set to True, e.g. `my_model`
      `torchao_config` (TorchAOBaseConfig): configuration for torchao quantization, full list: https://docs.pytorch.org/ao/main/api_ref_quantization.html#inference-apis-for-quantize
      `push_to_hub` (bool): whether to push the checkpoint to huggingface hub or save locally
    """
    # first merge the lora weights
    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]        = self
    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
    arguments["save_method"]  = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["torchao_config"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    from transformers import AutoModel, AutoTokenizer, TorchAoConfig
    from torchao import quantize_
    if torchao_config is None:
        from torchao.quantization import Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig
        torchao_config = Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig()
    quantization_config = TorchAoConfig(quant_type = torchao_config)

    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(arguments["save_directory"])
    if HAS_TORCH_DTYPE:
        kwargs = {"torch_dtype" : "auto"}
    else:
        kwargs = {"dtype" : "auto"}
    model = AutoModel.from_pretrained(
        arguments["save_directory"],
        device_map = "auto",
        quantization_config = quantization_config,
        **kwargs,
    )

    torchao_save_directory = save_directory + "-torchao"

    if push_to_hub:
        if token is None and push_to_hub: token = get_token()
        # torchao does not support safe_serialization right now
        model.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization = False, token = token)
        tokenizer.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, token = token)
    else:
        model.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization=False)
        tokenizer.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory)
    pass
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass


def not_implemented_save(*args, **kwargs):
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth: Sorry GGUF is currently not supported for vision models!")
pass

    model,

# llama.cpp specific targets - all takes 90s. Below takes 60s

# Check environments

{

def print_quantization_methods():
    for key, value in ALLOWED_QUANTS.items():
        print(f'"{key}"  ==> {value}')
    pass
pass


def check_if_sentencepiece_model(model, temporary_location = "_unsloth_sentencepiece_temp"):
    if not hasattr(model, "_saved_temp_tokenizer"): return False

    temp_tokenizer = model._saved_temp_tokenizer
    sentencepiece_model = False
    file_location = os.path.join(temporary_location, temp_tokenizer.name_or_path)
    created_folder = False
    if not os.path.exists(file_location):
        created_folder = True
        os.makedirs(file_location)
    pass
    temp_tokenizer.save_pretrained(file_location)
    if os.path.isfile(f"{file_location}/tokenizer.model"):
        sentencepiece_model = True
    pass
    if created_folder:
        shutil.rmtree(file_location, ignore_errors = True)
    return sentencepiece_model
pass


def _free_cached_model(model):
    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)

    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)

            logger.warning_once(
                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
            )

            delete_strategy.execute()
        pass
    pass
pass


def _merge_lora(layer, name):

    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        # W = W.t()

        if A is not None:
            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
            # W += sAB
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        pass
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
pass


def fast_save_pickle(shard, name):
    # Use this if # CPUs is <= 2
    print(f"Unsloth: Saving {name}...")
    torch.save(
        shard,
        name,
        # HIGHEST_PROTOCOL seems to not work with Pytorch!
        # pickle_module   = pickle,
        # pickle_protocol = pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL,
    )
    return
